quick_facts.label: What it is
quick_facts.value: NEET-SS (National Eligibility-cum-Entrance Test—Super Specialty) is NBEMS’s single eligibility-cum-ranking entrance examination for admission to DM, MCh and DrNB Super Specialty courses.

quick_facts.label: Exam authority
quick_facts.value: The National Board of Examinations in Medical Sciences (NBEMS) conducts NEET-SS.

quick_facts.label: Who can apply
quick_facts.value: Applicants need a recognised MD/MS/DNB qualification (or equivalent) or must obtain it by the session-specific cut-off date, and their prior qualification must be an eligible feeder specialty for the chosen super-specialty course/group.

quick_facts.label: 2026 timing
quick_facts.value: NBEMS’s April 29, 2026 notice lists NEET-SS 2026 tentatively for December 11–12, 2026, with January 31, 2027 as the tentative prior-entry-qualification cut-off. NBEMS states that exact dates will be set out in the later Information Bulletin.

quick_facts.label: Recent exam pattern reference
quick_facts.value: In NEET-SS 2025, the computer-based, group-based paper had 150 MCQs over 150 minutes, arranged as three time-bound 50-question/50-minute sections; correct answers received +4 and incorrect answers incurred −1.

quick_facts.label: Counselling route
quick_facts.value: For the 2025 admission session, qualified candidates participated in national-level common counselling for DM, MCh and DrNB Super Specialty seats conducted by MCC under DGHS. Counselling rules and seat availability are session-specific.

sections.heading: Purpose and scope
paragraphs: NEET-SS is the national gateway examination for postgraduate doctors seeking entry to super-specialty training. NBEMS describes it as the prescribed single entrance examination for DM, MCh and DrNB Super Specialty programmes under section 61(2) of the National Medical Commission Act, 2019.

The scope stated by NBEMS includes DM/MCh courses across the country—including private, deemed-university and Armed Forces Medical Services institutions—and DrNB Super Specialty courses other than direct six-year DrNB programmes. NBEMS’s overview separately identifies AIIMS, PGIMER, JIPMER and NIMHANS as outside NEET-SS centralised admissions for DM/MCh seats; candidates should therefore confirm the applicable admission route for a particular institution and programme.
bullets: A qualifying NEET-SS score is used for admission to the applicable DM/MCh/DrNB Super Specialty pathway; it is not itself an admission offer.

The current public schedule notice describes the December 11–12, 2026 examination dates as tentative. Watch for the 2026 Information Bulletin before treating any date, eligibility cut-off, fee or procedure as final.

sections.heading: Eligibility: postgraduate qualification and feeder specialty
paragraphs: The core eligibility model is qualification-based: candidates must hold a recognised MD, MS, DNB or equivalent postgraduate qualification—or be likely to hold it by the notified cut-off—and must meet the feeder-specialty requirement for the super-specialty they want to pursue. The exact feeder mappings are published in the session Information Bulletin and can differ by course/group.

The 2025 bulletin required NMC or State Medical Council registration for Indian candidates, with documentary proof at the test centre. It also stated that foreign nationals and OCI cardholders could take the examination without NMC registration, subject to the provisions described for temporary registration if admitted. These details should be reconfirmed against the 2026 bulletin.

A candidate who is already pursuing or has joined a DM, MCh or DrNB doctoral course through NEET-SS was not eligible in the 2025 rules to seek a DrNB Super Specialty seat through that session for the prescribed duration of the earlier course, irrespective of resignation or discontinuation.
bullets: Check the course-specific feeder list rather than assuming that every MD/MS/DNB specialty qualifies for every group.
For planning only: the April 29, 2026 NBEMS notice gives January 31, 2027 as the tentative cut-off for completing the prior-entry qualification for NEET-SS 2026.

sections.heading: Exam format and scoring
paragraphs: NEET-SS 2025 was delivered as a computer-based examination and organised by question-paper group. Candidates could choose one or more groups only where their broad-specialty qualification was an eligible feeder qualification. NBEMS used 15 groups in 2025, with a common question paper for the super-specialty courses covered by each group.

The 2025 paper contained 150 multiple-choice questions to be attempted in 150 minutes. It was split into Sections A, B and C, each allowing 50 minutes for 50 questions. A candidate could not move forward early, or revisit and change answers from a completed section. The scoring rule was +4 for a correct response, −1 for an incorrect response, and 0 for an unattempted question.

For most 2025 groups, questions were drawn from the general/basic and subspecialty components of the primary feeder broad specialty at postgraduate-exit level. Critical Care Medicine and Medical Oncology were separately examined on their respective super-specialty topics. Pattern, groups and syllabus coverage must be verified from the 2026 bulletin when released.
bullets: The 2025 qualifying standard was the 50th percentile or above within each question-paper group-specific merit list.
NBEMS stated that group-wise merit lists were prepared from marks, without equating, scaling or normalisation; its published tie-break sequence began with fewer negative responses.

sections.heading: Application and admissions process
paragraphs: When NBEMS opens the session window, candidates apply online through the NEET-SS area of the NBEMS website and Applicant Login. In the 2025 process, applicants selected eligible question-paper group(s), supplied the requested personal and qualification details, uploaded a recent photograph, signature and thumb impression, selected an available test city, and completed online payment. The application was complete only after the payment status showed successful.

The 2025 fee was ₹3,500 per selected question-paper group, excluding any applicable payment-gateway charges; this is historical, session-specific context rather than a quote for NEET-SS 2026. The 2025 bulletin allowed certain edits only within a notified edit window and excluded key fields such as name, nationality, test city, mobile number and email from correction.

After result and qualification, the 2025 system used group-specific merit and MCC/DGHS common national counselling. Candidates could exercise choices only for the courses in their qualified group for which their own broad-specialty qualification was an eligible feeder. A score was valid only for that admission session and could not be carried forward.
bullets: Do not rely on the 2025 application dates: they were November 5–25, 2025 and are already past.
For NEET-SS 2026, NBEMS advises candidates to monitor its website for the Information Bulletin and online application form; the current December 2026 examination dates remain tentative.

sections.heading: Preparation approach
paragraphs: Build preparation around the specific question-paper group and feeder mapping, not simply the title of a desired DM/MCh/DrNB course. In the 2025 scheme, primary-feeder groups tested the postgraduate-exit curriculum of the relevant broad specialty, including general/basic and subspecialty components; Medical Oncology and Critical Care Medicine had specialty-topic papers. Start by reading the current bulletin’s group table and feeder annexures when the 2026 version becomes available.

Practise in the computer-based format and rehearse time management in three 50-minute blocks. This is especially important because the 2025 format prevented candidates from returning to a prior section after its allotted time elapsed. NBEMS also made a CBT demo test available to 2025 applicants; candidates should look for any comparable official familiarisation resource for the 2026 session.
bullets: Use the official Information Bulletin as the primary checklist for syllabus boundaries, eligible groups, image specifications, test-day requirements and deadline changes.
Apply only after checking that qualification, registration and documentary requirements will be met by the session’s notified cut-off date.

sections.heading: What qualifying leads to
paragraphs: A qualifying result makes a candidate eligible to enter the applicable counselling process; it does not automatically confer admission. For NEET-SS 2025, NBEMS declared candidates at or above the 50th percentile in their respective question-paper group qualified, and MCC/DGHS conducted the common national counselling for the covered DM, MCh and DrNB Super Specialty seats.

The practical outcome is a group-specific merit position and the ability to state preferences for seats that both fall within the qualified group and accept the candidate’s feeder qualification. Final allotment, reservation rules, seat matrix, reporting and admission verification are handled through the counselling/admitting process and vary by session.
bullets: The 2025 NEET-SS result was declared on January 23, 2026 for the 2025–26 admission session.
Candidates should use MCC’s official portal for the live counselling schedule, scheme, seat matrix and allotment notices once the relevant session opens.
